title: Educational foundation
dek: Kehan Dong's early educational journey laid the groundwork for his future work in entrepreneurship and education.
narrative: The foundation for Kehan Dong's work with early founders and unconventional paths began with his own educational experiences, which shaped his understanding of how people discover what they're capable of.

title: Inner Journey: Transformational work with founders
dek: Founded Inner Journey to help founders and leaders align inner development with outer achievement through conscious growth.
narrative: Kehan Dong founded Inner Journey as a transformational program for founders and leaders experiencing the cost of scaling without inner alignment. The program draws on 25+ years of experience and ancient wisdom, offering a holistic and measurable approach to conscious leadership.

Inner Journey focuses on three core transitions: from outer achievement to inner alignment, from self-mastery to conscious leadership, and from doing to being. The program has transformed over 1000 lives, helping founders shift from striving to flow.
proofPoints: 25+ years of experience in transformational work
Based on ancient wisdom with holistic and measurable approach
mapCaption: Inner Journey serves founders and leaders globally.

evidenceProfile.strength: Direct organizational website and public profile

title: Code of Love NGO: Social impact through education
dek: Founded Code of Love, an NGO focused on social impact and educational initiatives.
narrative: As founder of the Code of Love NGO, Kehan Dong extended his commitment to education and social impact beyond the entrepreneurial sphere. The organization represents his belief in creating environments that make early discovery possible for all.
mapCaption: Code of Love operates as a global NGO.

title: Angel investing in early-stage founders
dek: Active angel investor supporting people who start building earlier than they're supposed to, focusing on unconventional paths and long-term agency.
narrative: Kehan Dong's investment philosophy centers on supporting early founders who take unconventional paths. He works with people who start building before they're 'supposed to,' believing that the right environment can unlock early discovery of capability.
His investment approach integrates with his educational work, creating a comprehensive system around early founders, education, and long-term agency.
proofPoints: Focus on early-stage founders and unconventional paths
Integration of investment with educational systems
mapCaption: Angel investing across global startup ecosystems.

title: Startup Go Camp at Duke Kunshan University
dek: Co-founded and taught Startup Go Camp (SGC) at Duke Kunshan University, a 6-week intensive entrepreneurial course achieving 90.4/100 student rating.
narrative: In collaboration with Duke Kunshan University's Innovation and Entrepreneurship Initiative, Kehan Dong co-founded Startup Go Camp (SGC), a comprehensive entrepreneurial series course tailored for student entrepreneurs. The program provides guidance for the initial stage of entrepreneurship from 0 to 1.

The 6-week course covers entrepreneurial basics, market demand identification, user research, MVP development, business strategy, team management, and finding financing. Kehan combines classic business cases like Facebook's startup story with his own entrepreneurial experience.

A distinctive feature is the 4-hour weekly Office Hours, which Kehan considers crucial for high-quality interaction with students. The course achieved a remarkable 90.4/100 student satisfaction rating, with many students continuing to build their projects after completion.
proofPoints: 4-hour weekly Office Hours for personalized mentorship
mapCaption: Startup Go Camp at Duke Kunshan University in Kunshan, Jiangsu Province, China.

evidenceProfile.strength: Direct institutional article with detailed coverage
evidenceProfile.artifact: Ready for rich educational artifact cards

title: Current focus: Systems around early founders and education
dek: Building integrated systems around early founders, education, and long-term agency through investing, writing, and program development.
narrative: Kehan Dong's current work integrates three core activities: angel investing in early-stage founders, writing about entrepreneurship and education, and building systems that support unconventional paths to success.

His focus remains on the question: How do people discover what they're capable of early, and what environments make that possible? This inquiry drives his investment decisions, educational programs, and organizational development.
proofPoints: Active across investing, writing, and system building
Focus on early discovery and capability development
mapCaption: Current work spans global entrepreneurial and educational ecosystems.

evidenceProfile.strength: Direct personal website with clear current focus
